hypothesis: If a hybrid deep learning architecture combining a 2D CNN for surface encoding with a GRU-based sequence encoder coupled through a multi-head attention mechanism is used to model polymer-surface binding free energies, then it will outperform baseline DNN and alternative sequential encoder architectures in predicting adhesion because the attention mechanism explicitly captures the asymmetric, query-key interaction between polymer sequence features and spatially-encoded surface features that governs binding specificity.
